original_prompt: Create a robust barcode scanner test automation framework mocking Html5Qrcode. Write internal tests for MobileStockView (debouncing, duplicates, missing hardware selection) and MobileAppointmentView (successful fulfillment, hardware mismatch) using the existing vanilla JS testing utility.
rephrased_goal: Implement a vanilla JS hardware mock for Html5Qrcode that intercepts camera requests and allows manual scan injections. Write a comprehensive test suite in `src/tests/scanner.test.js` validating the barcode logic for MobileStockView and MobileAppointmentView, integrating it into the core test runner while handling async state and DOM cleanup.

tasks.description: Do-Pebble: Analyze test environment and views
subtasks_required: 1.2 Read src/tests/index.js to see integration points
tasks.analogy_reference: Look at hunting tools before throw

tasks.description: Do-Pebble: Implement Scanner Mock & Test Setup
subtasks_required: 2.3 Add start(), stop(), and global simulateScan() to the mock
tasks.analogy_reference: Make fake animal to practice spear
tasks.testing_criteria: Mock captures callbacks and executes simulateScan successfully.

subtasks_required: 3.1 Write Test A: Debounce & Duplicates (scan P-123 twice, assert length 1)
3.2 Write Test B: Invalid Hardware Assignment (prevent load without type)
tasks.analogy_reference: Throw spear at fake animal logic 1
tasks.testing_criteria: Tests pass correctly handling DOM and mock DB.

subtasks_required: 4.1 Write Test C: Successful Fulfillment (scan valid ID for catalog slot)
4.2 Write Test D: Hardware Mismatch (scan wrong ID, assert graceful rejection)
tasks.analogy_reference: Throw spear at fake animal logic 2
tasks.testing_criteria: Tests pass correctly asserting DOM state changes based on simulated scans.

subtasks_required: 5.1 Import and execute runScannerTests inside src/tests/index.js
tasks.analogy_reference: Put fake animal in main practice field

subtasks_required: Verify await/async delays handle Firebase lag
